method: Within-word sign-permutation null for word-initial incidence of sign 'A' (positional slot only), applied globally, per-site (sites with >=20 qualifying words), leave-one-site-out, and to 5 frequency-matched comparator signs. Linear B used ONLY as positive-control benchmark via a seeded balanced 5-way random split (no per-site LB metadata available).
verdict_rule_applied: (PC passed) AND A- significant (p<=0.05) in >=3 independent sites AND A- significant under leave-one-site-out AND A- clears >=2 more sites than median comparator sign. All four conjuncts TRUE.

positive_control.lb_partition: seeded balanced 5-way random split (seed=20240123); NO per-site/series/scribe metadata available in load_b_damos return
positive_control.pc_verdict: PASSED (machinery recovers a known cross-partition prefix and rejects a frequency-matched random sign)

key_findings: A- is word-initial 155/177 (87.6%) of its occurrences and 155/1369 (11.3%) of all >=2-sign words; global permutation null p=0.001 (1000 draws).

Positive control PASSED: LB prefix analogue A3 (94.5% initial, 110 occ) is significant in 5/5 seeded partitions; frequency-matched random sign QI (21% initial) is significant in 0/5. Machinery is informative.
A- is significant (raw p<=0.05) in 9 of 10 qualifying LA sites, and Holm-significant (family of 10) in the same 9; only Tylissos (n=21) fails.
A- survives leave-one-site-out: with the largest site (Haghia Triada, 694 words) removed, p=0.001 on the remaining 675 words.
A- clears 9 sites vs a median of 2 for the 5 next-most-initial comparator signs (KU=1, I=3, JA=3, DA=1, SA=2); A- clears 7 more than the median comparator, far exceeding the >=2 threshold.
All four conjuncts of the frozen A_PREFIX_CROSS_SITE_ROBUST rule are TRUE.
successor_hypotheses: H1 (L2/L3): Test whether A- is also word-initial in the SECOND position of multi-clause/administrative lines (line-initial vs word-initial) — does the prefix slot align with line boundaries?
H2 (L2/L3): Conditional on the following sign — is the A- slot followed by a restricted set of signs (suggesting a closed class of A-prefixed lexemes) or freely distributed?
H3 (L2/L3): Co-occurrence of A- with numeric/quantity tokens (t=='num') in the same line — is the A- slot associated with enumeration contexts (a possible determinative/heading role)?
H4 (L2/L3): Within-site stability over support type — partition Haghia Triada by 'support'/'context' field and re-test A-; does it hold across support types within the dominant site?
H5 (L2/L3): Bigram-prefix test — is the A-X bigram (first two signs) overrepresented vs the within-word permutation null, i.e. is there a fixed A-initial template beyond the single-slot effect?

H6 (L2/L3): Replicate the cross-site protocol for the one comparator that cleared 3 sites (I, JA) to determine whether any non-A sign also meets a weaker cross-site robustness bar, calibrating how 'special' A- truly is.
H7 (method): Replace the seeded random LB split with a real LB series/scribe partition if metadata can be reconstructed from DAMOS item ids, to remove the only soft spot in the positive control.
non_circular: Sign names (A, JA, KU, ...) are ANONYMOUS identifiers. No phonetic value, sound, meaning, language, or reading is assigned or implied to ANY sign. 'A-' denotes a POSITIONAL SLOT (word-initial occurrence of the sign token 'A') only. No known-value data is a model input on the LA side. Linear B is used ONLY as a positive-control benchmark for the machinery; because load_b_damos() exposes no per-site/series/scribe metadata, the LB partition is a seeded balanced 5-way random split (stated explicitly).
